title: Mari is irrelevant not character bashing
display_title: Mari is irrelevant not character bashing
wikitext:
[]: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by Lord ikari shinji » Mon Dec 09, 2013 5:39 pm honestly what is Mari purpose in this rebuild of the series?! In 2.22 she serves as Asuka replacement & in 3.33 she really didn't do anything really but provide comedic relief and save Shinji in the end. mari basically has no character development, I mean every character in RoE expect Shinji for the must part, has terrible development {i.e: Asuka, Misato, etc.} they are shadows of their former selfs and are probably total different characters in of itself and that's understand able but mari pretty much has no development. Honestly how can any one like her or really be a fan of her I don't know. Did Anno mainly add her for fan serves?! In my opinion, she a seems really irrelevant Discuss: Is Mari Makinami irrelevant to the plot of RoE/ evangelion as a hole? or is it to early to call(wait for Final to decide) Someone please enlighten me so I can learn to love her{Mari}

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Dec 09, 2013 5:48 pm Lord ikari Shinji wrote: [i]Is Mari Makinami irrelevant to the plot of RoE/ evangelion as a hole?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Dec 09, 2013 5:54 pm Maybe she provides a backdrop of normality against which to measure the disintegration of others. Alternatively, the idea of comic relief is a well-established concept, so there's the possibility that she is providing that to slightly ease the sympathetic suffering of the viewer. What about seeing her as a version of the Greek chorus, occasionally observing and commenting on the action around her without being much affected by it? I favour the theory that we still have more to learn about her. Or all of these in part, making her a rich and multi-faceted character.

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 09, 2013 6:19 pm Lord ikari Shinji wrote: honestly what is Mari purpose in this rebuild of the series?!

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by Redtophat » Mon Dec 09, 2013 8:45 pm Lord ikari Shinji wrote: Honestly how can any one like her or really be a fan of her I don't know.

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Dec 09, 2013 8:58 pm June wrote: Mari is the best Evangelion character.

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by Redtophat » Mon Dec 09, 2013 9:00 pm xanderkh wrote: Or better yet, she's supposed to represent the IDEAL Evangelion character, one who TRIVES in the Eva world, as a direct contrast to Shinji. She's confident, laughs and thrives in the face of danger, knows how to deal with the real world, KICKS ANGEL ASS EASILY, and even forms a STABLE partnership with Asuka of all people. She is in a sense the perfect Eva pilot. Basically, she's supposed to represent what kind of person is meant to thrive in the harsh world that is Evangelion, while opposed to the most Normal and least likely person to thrive in this harsh environment, which is Shinji.

[]: Re: Mari is irrelevant?! {not character bashing}             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Dec 09, 2013 9:12 pm June wrote: Yes! Xander yes! I knew you of all people would understand!

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Dec 09, 2013 9:38 pm Comic relief for now, I suppose. Honestly, I think Anno & Co. would agree that the inclusion of Mari wasn't particularly well thought out. They reeaally wanted to introduce a new character to shake things up, and granted, at the time the idea of a new addition to this almost legendary cast of characters seemed pretty mind-blowing. But they seem to have struggled enough just to come up with her basic personality, never mind finding a place for her in the story (which they've postponed twice now.) They're gonna come up with something eventually, but until then: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zZSFvntwgEQ

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Dec 09, 2013 10:42 pm {Thread Title LIES!}

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Tue Dec 10, 2013 2:22 am I dont really get threads like this. There is still a movie left people! There is clearly a lot more to Mari than meets the eye, and it will become clear. It may not be very transparent, but what in Eva is? The fact that Ritsuko has blonde hair and brown eyebrows didn't seem at all important until late in the series, for example. There will be more to Mari.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 10, 2013 2:40 am Th3Marauder wrote: I dont really get threads like this. There is still a movie left people! There is clearly a lot more to Mari than meets the eye, and it will become clear.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Dec 10, 2013 3:09 am Quite well, I'd say (having watched it).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 10, 2013 3:19 am pwhodges wrote: Quite well, I'd say (having watched it).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Dec 10, 2013 3:33 am True, but it was not about that. It seemed to me that she was better integrated than in the previous film, which was all that was needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Dec 10, 2013 4:14 am Mari is so irrelevant Anno doesn't give a fuck about her VA's takes. Maaya basically did one and done with Mari. While other characters did multiple takes before Anno was satisfied...says a lot really where he plans to go with her.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Dec 10, 2013 4:23 am pwhodges wrote: True, but it was not about that. It seemed to me that she was better integrated than in the previous film, which was all that was needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Dec 10, 2013 5:08 am Course Mari is relevant. Her Eva is pink.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Tue Dec 10, 2013 9:16 am I've said this before, in another one of the thousand threads and sections that bash Mari... Mari represents Anno's wife - someone who wasn't completely fucked in the head and helped him get through his depressive state. The allure of Eva to so many is that we, the viewers, connect with the depressed characters because we ourselves are depressed/have been depressed, so we think it's the greatest shit in the world. (Which it is.) I think the resistance to Mari is akin to the resistance against happy people when you're in a state of depression: "There's no WAY people can be happy like that all the time. Surely, everyone is sad and fucked up like I am!" But the truth is, there are genuinely happy people. Mari represents that. That's how Mari *breaks* Eva - she's the one and only person who isn't fucked up, and she, like her real-life counterpart (my interpretation, at least), is there to help the depressed male lead get through his problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Dec 10, 2013 9:58 am Mari is my spirit animal. Final will end with Mari defeating the MPEs or whatever and the Final Extreme Super Angel and surf her exploding Eva into the final adversary and cleanse Asuka of her angelic taint and ride off with her and Rei on a unicorn. The final image will be Shinji watching Mari ride off with his harem while a sad trombone plays. -FIN-
